member.name: M:HSMAdvisor.Calculators.ChamferMillEngagementCalculator.GetEffectiveDiameter(HSMAdvisor.Interfaces.IMillingCalculation,System.Double)
member.summary: Effective diameter at axial engagement DOC for tapered tools. LeadAngle: 90° = straight flute (constant diameter = cut.Diameter) <90° = positive taper (smaller at tip, larger up the flute) >90° = negative taper (larger at tip, smaller up the flute) DOC is axial engagement length from the tip.

member.name: M:HSMAdvisor.Calculators.ChamferMillEngagementCalculator.ComputeIdealCut(HSMAdvisor.Interfaces.IMillingCalculation,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary:
Calculates Ideal_DOC and Ideal_WOC for a chamfer mill.
Definitions: - baseDoc/baseWoc: preset-only recommendation ("free" values) - idealDoc/idealWoc: final recommendation after applying user constraints
Constraints rules: - If neither requested: ideal == base - If one requested: honor it, recompute the other only if that changes engagement (side milling only WOC matters) - If both requested: honor both; engagement delta goes into Material_Engagement_Factor (and tool_load_feed_factor inverse)
Geometry: - Slotting: WOC must equal effective diameter at DOC (forced)

member.name: M:HSMAdvisor.Calculators.EndMillEngagementCalculator.GetEffectiveDiameter(HSMAdvisor.Interfaces.IMillingCalculation,System.Double)
member.summary: Effective diameter at a given axial engagement (DOC) for tapered tools. LeadAngle: 90° = straight flute (constant diameter = cut.Diameter) <90° = positive taper (smaller at tip, larger up the flute) >90° = negative taper (larger at tip, smaller up the flute) DOC is axial engagement length from the tip.

member.name: M:HSMAdvisor.Calculators.EndMillEngagementCalculator.ComputeIdealCut(HSMAdvisor.Interfaces.IMillingCalculation,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Calculates Ideal_DOC and Ideal_WOC for a cut, optionally overriding DOC/WOC. Tool_Type values are fractions of a reference diameter: - normally cut.Diameter - for LeadAngle > 90, diameter at Flute_Len (negative taper, smaller at flute end) Slotting WOC is limited to effective diameter at current DOC & LeadAngle.

member.name: T:HSMAdvisor.Calculators.ThreadMillEngagementCalculator
member.summary: Engagement calculator specifically for thread mill tools. Thread mills have unique engagement requirements based on thread pitch and flute length.

member.name: M:HSMAdvisor.Calculators.ThreadMillEngagementCalculator.ComputeIdealCut(HSMAdvisor.Interfaces.IMillingCalculation,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary:
Calculates Ideal_DOC and Ideal_WOC for a thread mill cut.
Base DOC = Tool_Type.Side_doc × Flute_Len Base WOC = Thread_Pitch × 0.701
The engagement_override factor (material toughness, deflection) adjusts both DOC and WOC, allowing material properties to scale the engagement appropriately.
member.param: Requested width of cut (0 for default)
Requested depth of cut (0 for default)
Material/deflection factor override (scales both DOC and WOC)

member.name: M:HSMAdvisor.Calculators.Calc_Mill.Calculate_Effective_SFM_Diameter(HSMAdvisor.Interfaces.IMillingCalculation,System.Double)
member.summary: Calculate effective diameter for endmill operations considering corner radius and lead angle

member.name: M:HSMAdvisor.Calculators.Calc_Mill.Calculate_Axial_Chip_Thinning(HSMAdvisor.Interfaces.IMillingCalculation,System.Double)
member.summary: Calculate axial chip thinning factor for endmill operations

member.name: M:HSMAdvisor.Modules.Calculators.ChipEvacModel.ChipSpaceAreaPerMm(System.Double,System.Int32)
member.summary: Approx chip-space cross-sectional area (mm^2), i.e. chip volume per 1mm of axial length. Multiply by axial length (mm) to get chip-space volume (mm^3). D in mm. N = number of flutes.

member.name: M:HSMAdvisor.Modules.Calculators.FluteRecommendation.GetRecommendedFluteRange(HSMAdvisorDatabase.ToolDataBase.Enums.ToolTypes,System.Double)
member.summary: Returns recommended flute count range (min,max) for the given material group. Based on chip evacuation vs tool strength for each material family.

member.name: M:HSMAdvisor.Modules.Calculators.GumminessModel.GetGumminessFactor(System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Returns gumminess factor in [0..1]. 0 = brittle/crumbly chips (easy evacuation), 1 = stringy/adhesive (packs flutes). Uses your material "group" plus hardness as a small adjustment.

member.name: M:HSMAdvisor.Modules.Calculators.MaterialDocAdjustmentV2.Calculate_Material_Flute_N_DOC_Adjustment_Factor(System.Double,System.Int32,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary:
Returns DOC adjustment factor based on: - chip evacuation capacity vs 3-flute baseline (same diameter) - gumminess (0..1) derived from your material group + HB
Standard: 3-flute => factor = 1 (for same D and same material). >1 means "can go deeper", <1 means "should go shallower".

member.name: M:HSMAdvisor.Module1.NormalizeDecimalToCurrentCulture(System.String)
member.summary: Normalizes the decimal separator in a numeric string to match the current culture's formatting conventions.
member.remarks: This method removes group separators and normalizes the decimal separator to the one used by the current culture. It does not attempt to parse or validate the numeric value. If both the culture's decimal separator and a common alternate separator are present in the input, the method returns the input unchanged to avoid ambiguity.
member.param: The input string representing a numeric value to be normalized. May contain alternate decimal or group separators.
member.returns: A string with the decimal separator replaced to match the current culture. If both possible decimal separators are present, or if the input is null, empty, or whitespace, the original input is returned.
